headline: OpenAI IPO delayed by Apple threat?
summary: Host Kenny Polcari, Hennion & Walsh CIO Kevin Mahn, and Neostellar Capital Corp Principal Willy Lee break down Apple's intellectual property lawsuit against OpenAI, how it exposes Apple's AI vulnerabilities, and why the legal battle could push the highly anticipated IPO to 2027.

content.title: OpenAI IPO delayed by Apple threat?
content.description: Host Kenny Polcari, Hennion & Walsh CIO Kevin Mahn, and Neostellar Capital Corp Principal Willy Lee break down Apple's intellectual property lawsuit against OpenAI, how it exposes Apple's AI vulnerabilities, and why the legal battle could push the highly anticipated IPO to 2027.
content.summary: Host Kenny Polcari, Hennion & Walsh CIO Kevin Mahn, and Neostellar Capital Corp Principal Willy Lee break down Apple's intellectual property lawsuit against OpenAI, how it exposes Apple's AI vulnerabilities, and why the legal battle could push the highly anticipated IPO to 2027.

headline: Intel results to test if AI-fueled rally has room to run
summary: Intel's results on Thursday will show whether the American chip icon has the numbers to back a Wall Street rally that has ‌sent its shares nearly three times higher this year, as its turnaround ‌push wins over investors and the AI buildout powers demand.  Investors are also eager for details on potential new customers for its contract manufacturing business, which has shown growing traction with deals including a ‌Tesla tie-up.  • Among the biggest ⁠questions for Intel is a potential deal to manufacture processors for Apple that U.S. President Donald Trump announced in April but neither ⁠company has confirmed.

content.title: Intel results to test if AI-fueled rally has room to run
content.summary: Intel's results on Thursday will show whether the American chip icon has the numbers to back a Wall Street rally that has ‌sent its shares nearly three times higher this year, as its turnaround ‌push wins over investors and the AI buildout powers demand.  Investors are also eager for details on potential new customers for its contract manufacturing business, which has shown growing traction with deals including a ‌Tesla tie-up.  • Among the biggest ⁠questions for Intel is a potential deal to manufacture processors for Apple that U.S. President Donald Trump announced in April but neither ⁠company has confirmed.

headline: Samsung unveils wider foldable phone, hikes prices as chip costs rise
summary: Samsung Electronics on Wednesday unveiled a passport-sized foldable phone and raised prices of two updated foldable models, as it grapples ‌with rising memory chip prices while bracing for a new threat from Apple ‌in the niche but growing segment.  The South Korean technology company pioneered foldable smartphones but its dominance is set to ​be challenged by Apple which analysts expect this year to launch a foldable phone with a screen similar in size to an iPad mini.  "Strategically, this is one of Samsung's most important foldable launches," said Francisco Jeronimo, vice president at researcher IDC.

content.title: Samsung unveils wider foldable phone, hikes prices as chip costs rise
content.summary: Samsung Electronics on Wednesday unveiled a passport-sized foldable phone and raised prices of two updated foldable models, as it grapples ‌with rising memory chip prices while bracing for a new threat from Apple ‌in the niche but growing segment.  The South Korean technology company pioneered foldable smartphones but its dominance is set to ​be challenged by Apple which analysts expect this year to launch a foldable phone with a screen similar in size to an iPad mini.  "Strategically, this is one of Samsung's most important foldable launches," said Francisco Jeronimo, vice president at researcher IDC.

thumbnail.caption: FILE PHOTO: Illustration shows Samsung Electronics logo

headline: Vanguard’s VIG Dividend ETF’s Top Stock Is… Broadcom? Here’s Why

headline: Apple-OpenAI lawsuit won’t derail IPO, but hangs over io Products — lawyer
summary: Investing.com -- Apple’s trade secret lawsuit named OpenAI and io Products directly as defendants, alongside two former Apple employees, but the litigation is unlikely to derail OpenAI’s IPO plans, according to Ross Carmel, a partner and IPO expert at Sichenzia Ross Ference Carmel LLP.
